project.name: Internal Dashboard Benchmark Case
project.purpose: Plan an internal customer-account dashboard before implementation so the customer team can decide which accounts need attention without downstream actors inventing users, account signals, data boundaries, or meeting acceptance criteria.

non_goals.title: Do not implement, scaffold, or run a dashboard from this benchmark case.
Do not connect live customer systems, write back to CRM, automate account workflows, or create downstream runtime state.
Do not run downstream agents, model APIs, shell adapters, queues, PR automation, or release automation.

capabilities.title: Capture the vague dashboard request as planning state.
Block readiness while dashboard intent is unresolved.

requirements.title: The plan must record the original dashboard request, visible assumptions, non-goals, risks, and blocker questions without treating them as implementation instructions.
The readiness gate must remain blocked while the primary actor, attention definition, source systems, privacy boundary, and planning-meeting evidence are unresolved.

decisions.title: Treat the dashboard request as planning evidence, not build scope.
Use the benchmark workspace only for ni CLI readiness proof.

risks.title: A downstream actor could build around guessed users, metrics, charts, deadlines, and success criteria.
risks.mitigation: Keep actor, attention-definition, source-system, and acceptance questions as blocker open questions until the user answers or explicitly defers them with rationale.

risks.title: Customer account signals could be exposed to the wrong audience if the privacy and access-control boundary is guessed.
risks.mitigation: Block lock until allowed source systems, required fields, data freshness, privacy review, and access-control constraints are explicit.

risks.title: Account prioritization could be incorrect or stale if needs-attention signals are undefined.
risks.mitigation: Block lock until needs-attention signals, freshness expectations, and review evidence for planning-meeting use are explicit.

evaluations.title: Planning capture review.
evaluations.method: Review docs/plan/** and .ni/contract.json to confirm the vague dashboard request is represented as planning state, not executable dashboard implementation scope.

evaluations.title: Blocked readiness proof.
evaluations.method: Run go run ./cmd/ni status --dir examples/benchmark-report/cases/internal-dashboard/workspace --proof --next-questions and verify it reports BLOCKED with blocker open questions before downstream work starts.

open_questions.title: Who exactly is the primary dashboard user, and what decision should the dashboard support?
What does needs attention mean in observable account signals?
Which source systems, account fields, freshness rules, privacy constraints, and access controls may v0 use?
What acceptance evidence must be ready for the next planning meeting?
